question: Should a cash withdrawal count as External Spend immediately, or as a transfer to a tracked cash account until later cash spending is recorded?
context: Resolved by explicit owner approval on 2026-07-21. V1 has no tracked-cash account: a cash withdrawal is recognized as spend when it leaves the bank account, preventing later unrecorded cash use from understating actuals.
decision: Owner decision on 2026-07-21: a cash withdrawal is immediate External Spend and Budget Actual in its Effective Date period; it is not an owned-account transfer because v1 has no tracked-cash account. Any separately recorded withdrawal fee remains ordinary spend. A linked reversal or refund offsets the withdrawal under the normal relationship rules. Later use of the same withdrawn cash is not recorded again as additional spend, preventing double counting.
